moduleC.title: Multi-Agent Dashboard
readings.title: Readings
readings.section1.title: EEW System Design Trade-offs
readings.section1.content: EEW systems face a fundamental trade-off between blind zone radius and reliability. Denser station networks reduce the blind zone but increase cost and maintenance burden. Taiwan compressed lead time from 102s to 7s across 3000+ channels. ML models further improve intensity estimation accuracy, but introduce model-based false alarm risks.
readings.section2.title: Traditional vs Machine Learning
readings.section2.content: Traditional algorithms rely on manually tuned thresholds and empirical formulas. Recent deep learning breakthroughs in phase picking have been remarkable. SeisWav2Vec 2.0 uses self-supervised learning to extract features from unlabeled data, achieving state-of-the-art performance with minimal labeled data.
readings.section3.title: Technological Limits: False Alarms / Missed Events / Tsunami
readings.section3.content: When two earthquakes occur within seconds (e.g., 4s apart), EEW may process only the first and miss the second, larger event. False alarms can arise from distant earthquake wave patterns. Tsunami warning additionally requires depth, seafloor displacement, and wave height estimation. Taiwan has 6 tsunami warning zones.
readings.section4.title: AI/LLM Technical Details
readings.section4.content: LEM (Large Earthquake Model) adapts Wav2Vec 2.0's speech processing approach to seismic waveform analysis. SeisWav2Vec 2.0 uses contrastive learning with a feature encoder, 12-layer Transformer context encoder, and masked prediction tasks. The multi-agent system integrates my_agent (monitoring), seismo (modeling), seismo_agent (data processing), and secondary (integration).
